hypothesis: Incorporating a differentiable, charge-conserving partial-charge head coupled to an Ewald-summed long-range Coulomb term into the MACE model will reduce NVE energy drift by ≥50% and force MAE by ≥15% on solvated ionic biomolecular systems (~1000 atoms, 0.15 M NaCl) relative to the current short-range models, while increasing JAX-MD per-step runtime by ≤25%.
